title: torch-npu 2.5.1 incompatible with NumPy 2.x due to removed np.float_
problem_description: torch-npu 2.5.1 internally references np.float_ (alias removed in NumPy 2.0). When pip installs the latest numpy (2.2.6) by default, this causes an AttributeError or similar failure in torch-npu internals. The dependency is not pinned in torch-npu's metadata.
rough_fix_approach: Pin numpy<2.0 when installing torch-npu 2.5.1: pip install 'numpy<2.0'. For CANN 8.1.RC1 + torch-npu 2.5.1, numpy==1.26.x is the confirmed working version.
artifact_evidence: validated/phase_5_validation_canonical.json: loop_history shows iteration 1 required fix including numpy downgrade
reasoning: NumPy 2.x breaking changes affect many packages, but torch-npu 2.5.1 specifically depends on the removed np.float_ alias. This is a known migration pitfall that occurs the moment a fresh venv installs the latest numpy. Easily preempted by pinning.
